--- title: Two-Factor ANOVA subtitle: Sample Problems # author: Guenther, Benjamin A. project: type: website output-dir: docs format: html: html-math-method: mathjax self-contained-math: false embed-resources: false jupyter: python3 --- # About the factorial ANOVA problems use the `FactorialData` class which requires: * a tuple to specify the levels of each factor - (factor A, factor B) * the sample size per group - all groups will have the same sample size Each of the problems below sets factor levels randomly with 2 or 3 levels. Per-group sample size is randomly set between 5 and 15. An example funciton call is included below ::: {#ef559678 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} levels_factor_a = random.randint(2,3) levels_factor_b = random.randint(2,3) n = random.randint(5,15) FactorialData(design = (levels_factor_a, levels_factor_b), group_n = n).factorial_ANOVA() ``` ::: # Problem 1 Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with $\alpha = {0.01}$ to analyze the following data


Full Group Summary Data
$T_{A1B1} = {319} \quad M_{A1B1} = {29.0}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {52.0} \quad n_{A1B1} = {11}$
$T_{A1B2} = {238} \quad M_{A1B2} = {21.64}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {18.55} \quad n_{A1B2} = {11}$
$T_{A2B1} = {226} \quad M_{A2B1} = {20.55}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {54.73} \quad n_{A2B1} = {11}$
$T_{A2B2} = {304} \quad M_{A2B2} = {27.64}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {44.55} \quad n_{A2B2} = {11}$
$T_{A3B1} = {227} \quad M_{A3B1} = {20.64}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {68.55} \quad n_{A3B1} = {11}$
$T_{A3B2} = {278} \quad M_{A3B2} = {25.27}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {84.18} \quad n_{A3B2} = {11}$
$\Sigma X^2 = {39478}$
$G = {1592}$

Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations

Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: $df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}$

$df_{total} = N - 1$

$df_{total} = {66} - 1$

$df_{total} = {65}$


$df_{between} = k - 1$

$df_{between} = {6} - 1$

$df_{between} = {5}$


$df_{within} = N - K$

$df_{within} = {66} - {6}$

$df_{within} = {60}$


Calculate the Sum of Squares: $SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}$

$SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}$

$SS_{total} = {39478} - \frac{1592^2}{66}$

$SS_{total} = {39478} - \frac{2534464}{66}$

$SS_{total} = {39478} - {38400.97}$

$SS_{total} = {1077.03}$


$SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}$

$SS_{within} = {52.0 + 18.55 + 54.73 + 44.55 + 68.55 + 84.18}$

$SS_{within} = {322.56}$


$SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}$

$SS_{between} = {1077.03} - {322.56}$

$SS_{between} = {754.47}$


note: the other way to calculate $SS_{betwen}$ is:

$SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}$




Stage 2 calculations for $Factor_A$

Summary Data for Factor_A
$T_{A_1} = {557} \quad n_{A_1} = {22}$
$T_{A_2} = {530} \quad n_{A_2} = {22}$
$T_{A_3} = {505} \quad n_{A_3} = {22}$

State the Hypotheses
$H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3$
$H_1:$ At least one mean is different

partition df for $Factor_A$

$df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1$

$df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1$

$df_{Factor_A} = {2}$


The decision criteria:

$F_{crit} = {4.98}, \alpha = {0.01}$

Partition the SS for $Factor_A$

Calculate $SS_{Factor_A}$

$\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}$

$SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{557}^2}{22} + \frac{{530}^2}{22} + \frac{{505}^2}{22}} - \frac{{1592}^2}{66}$

$SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{310249}}{22} + \frac{{280900}}{22} + \frac{{255025}}{22}} - \frac{{2534464}}{66}$

$SS_{Factor_A} = {{14102.23} + {12768.18} + {11592.05}} - {38400.97}$

$SS_{Factor_A} = {38462.46} - {38400.97}$

$SS_{Factor_A} = {61.49}$

Mean Squares for ${Factor_A}$

$MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}$

$MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{61.49}{2}$

$MS_{Factor_A} = {30.74}$

Calculate the F-Ratio for ${Factor_A}$

$F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}$

$F_{Factor_A} = \frac{30.74}{5.38}$

$F_{Factor_A} = {5.71}$


Calculate $\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A$

$\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}$

$\eta_p^2 = \frac{61.49}{{1077.03} - {34.91} - {658.07}}$

$\eta_p^2 = \frac{61.49}{384.05}$

$\eta_p^2 = {0.16}$

The results:

reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,

$F({2}, {60}) = {5.71}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.16}$



Stage 2 calculations for $Factor_B$

Summary Data for Factor_B
$T_{B_1} = {772} \quad n_{B_1} = {33}$
$T_{B_2} = {820} \quad n_{B_2} = {33}$

State the Hypotheses
$H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2$
$H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2$

partition df for $Factor_B$

$df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1$

$df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1$

$df_{Factor_B} = {1}$


The decision criteria:

$F_{crit} = {7.08}, \alpha = {0.01}$

Partition the SS for $Factor_B$

Calculate $SS_{Factor_B}$

$\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}$

$SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{772}^2}{33} + \frac{{820}^2}{33}} - \frac{{1592}^2}{66}$

$SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{595984}}{33} + \frac{{672400}}{33}} - \frac{{2534464}}{66}$

$SS_{Factor_B} = {{18060.12} + {20375.76}} - {38400.97}$

$SS_{Factor_B} = {38435.88} - {38400.97}$

$SS_{Factor_B} = {34.91}$

Mean Squares for ${Factor_B}$

$MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}$

$MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{34.91}{1}$

$MS_{Factor_B} = {34.91}$

Calculate the F-Ratio for ${Factor_B}$

$F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}$

$F_{Factor_B} = \frac{34.91}{5.38}$

$F_{Factor_B} = {6.49}$


Calculate $\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B$

$\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}$

$\eta_p^2 = \frac{34.91}{{1077.03} - {61.49} - {658.07}}$

$\eta_p^2 = \frac{34.91}{357.47}$

$\eta_p^2 = {0.1}$

The results:

fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,

$F({1}, {60}) = {6.49}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.1}$



Stage 2 calculations for the $AxB$ interaction


State the Hypotheses
$H_0:$ There is no AxB interation
$H_1:$ There is an AxB interaction

Partition df for the interaction

$df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}$

$df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}$

$df_{AxB} = {2}$



The decision criteria:

$F_{crit} = {4.98}, \alpha = {0.01}$

Partition the SS for $AxB$

Calculate $SS_{AxB}$

$SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}$

$SS_{AxB} = {754.47} - {61.49} - {34.91}$

$SS_{AxB} = {658.07}$

Mean Squares for ${AxB}$

$MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}$

$MS_{AxB} = \frac{658.07}{2}$

$MS_{AxB} = {329.04}$

Calculate the F-Ratio for ${AxB}$

$F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}$

$F_{AxB} = \frac{329.04}{5.38}$

$F_{AxB} = {61.16}$


Calculate $\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB$

$\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}$

$\eta_p^2 = \frac{658.07}{{1077.03} - {61.49} - {34.91}}$

$\eta_p^2 = \frac{658.07}{980.63}$

$\eta_p^2 = {0.67}$

The results:

reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,

$F({2}, {60}) = {61.16}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.67}$

# Problem 3 Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with $\alpha = {0.05}$ to analyze the following data
